what_it_does: Defines a coordinate system of doc:// and area:// addresses for the guided-review-surface build documents and directory regions, and anchors full-coverage sweeps that collect how each area relates to the surface.
observations: Proposes two new address schemes, doc:// and area://, as extensions to the existing live address grammar.
Maps build artefacts to doc://grs/... identifiers, including this map itself as doc://grs/address-map.
Maps eight directory regions to area://... identifiers, from runtime and canvas to design-corpus.
Frames the map as dogfood: the live address grammar currently lacks document/directory schemes, and this file both uses and proposes the extension.
Defines a coverage anchor with four relationship lenses: use, touch/be touched by, unify/improve, and relate conceptually.
Mandates append-only findings anchored to the synthesis and criteria documents, with no filtering.
Specifies that coverage findings land at build-prep/guided-review-surface/findings/coverage/AREA-<name>.md.
standouts: The file is meta-infrastructure: it assigns addresses to its own build documents and to broad source-directory areas.
It explicitly proposes extending the project's live address grammar to cover documents and directory regions.
It insists on area-based full-coverage sweeps rather than query-driven searches.
conventions: Uses address identifiers paired with concrete file or directory paths.
Self-referential / dogfooding: applies the project's address-grammar concept to the build process itself.
Declares an append-only, grow-scope rule for findings.
Labels directory areas by functional organ or responsibility rather than by exact file lists.
concerns: The doc:// and area:// schemes are described as provisional and not yet formalized in the live grammar.
Directory-area descriptions bundle many responsibilities into single labels, which could blur boundaries during sweeps.
There is no visible versioning or change process for the address-to-path mappings.
notes: This file is the self-referential index that assigns doc:// and area:// coordinates to the guided-review-surface build artefacts and directory regions, then defines how to sweep each area for coverage.
questions: What is the exact grammar and validation rules for doc:// and area:// addresses beyond the examples given?
How are area boundaries decided and who maintains the mappings over time?
What does 'Tim's grow-scope law' specifically require in practice?
How will findings be merged or appended into Completion Criteria.md?
What is the relationship between area://design-corpus and the separate VAULT build-prep?
Is the address extension intended to be adopted in contracts/address.py or to remain build-local?
purpose_vs_actual: yes — the file supplies the coordinate mappings and coverage anchor described in its title and lead paragraph.
apparent_themes: Dogfooding the address grammar inside the build process
Area-based full-coverage sweeps instead of query-driven searches
Unification of scattered surfaces and organs through shared coordinates
intention_signals: It is trying to bootstrap a unified, addressable coordinate layer over both build documents and source-directory regions so the guided-review-surface build can systematically sweep every area for relevant connections without losing scope.
novelty: Proposes extending the project's live address grammar to cover documents and directory areas, and uses that extended grammar to address the build process itself.
contribution: A structured, addressable index linking build artefacts and directory areas to coverage rounds, plus the explicit sweep-by-area, append-findings method for scope-preserving build integration.
summary_for_embedding: BUILD-ADDRESS-MAP.md defines doc:// and area:// coordinates for the guided-review-surface build documents and source-directory regions, and anchors area-based coverage rounds that collect use, touch, unification, and conceptual relationships.
intention_for_embedding: It was written to give the guided-review-surface build a self-referential, full-coverage coordinate system so each directory area can be systematically swept for relevant findings.
